title: Font/source staleness + refresh/re-review process
context: Open fonts are updated; source licenses can change; formation sources may be superseded by new national curriculum guidance. Without a staleness-detection and refresh process, the project's license-clean and formation-accurate guarantees decay silently over time. Affected scripts need re-review when their upstream font or source changes — this is a recurring maintenance responsibility, not a one-time task.
objective: Define and document the staleness-detection and refresh process: how to detect upstream font updates and source/license changes, how affected scripts become maintenance tasks, and how the provenance-gate is re-run and reviewer sign-off is renewed on refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: Staleness-detection process documented: how to identify when an upstream font has a new version or when a source/license has changed (e.g. periodic check schedule or CI-triggered lookup).
Refresh workflow defined: when staleness is detected, the affected script's tasks become maintenance tasks with the provenance-gate re-run and reviewer sign-off renewed.
Process documented for font updates: how to vendor the new version, update the snapshot (SHA-256 + Wayback URL), run the gate, and re-review affected glyphs.
Ownership assigned: a named maintainer or rotating role is responsible for running the staleness check on a defined cadence.
resources: PLAN.md section 15 (Sustainability — staleness/refresh process), section 9 M4
gate-002 (provenance gate to re-run on refresh)
latin-009 (Latin model as the primary refresh subject)
output: A committed staleness-detection and refresh-process document (docs/refresh-process.md or similar), defining cadence, ownership, and the gate-re-run workflow — licensed CC-BY-4.0.
